channel.title: The Sentinel Review — National Security Feed
channel.description: Automated national security news aggregation. Updated every 8 hours.

item.title: 🌏 South Korea Getting Nuclear Submarines Is A Huge Deal | The War Zone
item.description: Nuclear propulsion will give South Korea more capable subs, but it will also lay the groundwork for a future sea-based nuclear deterrent option. The post South Korea Getting Nuclear Submarines Is A Huge Deal appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🌍 Why the U.S. Should Wind Down Military Aid to Israel | Foreign Policy
item.description: Washington should no longer be liable for Israeli misdeeds.

item.title: 🏴 Banned Russian Submunitions Found After Mali’s Military Announces Airstrikes | Bellingcat
item.description: This investigation is a collaboration between Bellingcat and Jeune Afrique. You can read Jeune Afrique’s article in French here. Unexploded Russian-made cluster munition bomblets, as well as damage consistent with bomblet impacts, have been found in a village in northern Mali –…

item.title: 🌏 South Korea outlines nuclear-powered submarine plan | Breaking Defense
item.description: South Korea’s Ministry of National Defense expects its first nuclear-powered submarine to enter service in the mid-2030s.

item.title: 🔐 Air Force sees another year delay for next-gen engines | Breaking Defense
item.description: The Next Generation Adaptive Propulsion program is now projected to complete prototyping work in 2031, a cumulative delay of three years compared to earlier projections.

item.title: 🏴 Understanding the Value of Ukrainian Railways | War on the Rocks
item.description: Welcome to The Ukraine Compass, a weekly digest of Ukrainian commentary and analysis from across the political spectrum only for War on the Rocks members. Each Monday, we bring you a curated selection of articles from Ukrainian media offering insight into how Ukrainians…

item.title: 🏴 Ground robots in Latvia and the history of manned-unmanned teaming | Breaking Defense
item.description: This week on The Break Out, we travel to eastern Europe for drone testing with NATO troops before briefly reviewing the history of unmanned systems.

item.title: 🏴 Report: US to cut strategic bombers and warships available to NATO in a crisis | Defense News
item.description: The number of U.S. fighter jets is set to fall by a third, Spiegel cited U.S. envoy Alexander Velez-Green as saying during the closed-door meeting.

item.title: 📍 Lipsky quoted in a Bloomberg article explaining that stablecoins will not uphold the dollar’s outsized global role on their own, with its fundamental pillars still laying with trust in institutions. | Atlantic Council
item.description: Read the full article here. The post Lipsky quoted in a Bloomberg article explaining that stablecoins will not uphold the dollar’s outsized global role on their own, with its fundamental pillars still laying with trust in institutions. appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 🕵️ Chhangani’s research featured in a Financial Times article on the uptick in transaction value settled on CIPS in March amidst the Iran war. | Atlantic Council
item.description: Read the full article here. The post Chhangani’s research featured in a Financial Times article on the uptick in transaction value settled on CIPS in March amidst the Iran war. appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 📍 Nikoladze published in the inaugural volume of the Journal of Geoeconomics on Chinese economic coercion and the case of trade restrictions against Australia from 2020–2024. | Atlantic Council
item.description: Read the full article here. The post Nikoladze published in the inaugural volume of the Journal of Geoeconomics on Chinese economic coercion and the case of trade restrictions against Australia from 2020–2024. appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 🏴 Nikoladze and Bhusari cited by an article in Northwestern Journal of International Law & Business on Russian gold holdings and the side effects of Russian reliance on the renminbi. | Atlantic Council
item.description: Read the full article here. The post Nikoladze and Bhusari cited by an article in Northwestern Journal of International Law & Business on Russian gold holdings and the side effects of Russian reliance on the renminbi. appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 📍 Lichfield cited in a Bloomberg article on how an agreed upon G7 diagnosis of global imbalances could begin a collaborative strategy. | Atlantic Council
item.description: Read the full article here. The post Lichfield cited in a Bloomberg article on how an agreed upon G7 diagnosis of global imbalances could begin a collaborative strategy. appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 📍 Lipsky cited in a New York Times article on the specter of Trump’s tariffs over G7 ministers meeting. | Atlantic Council
item.description: Read the full article here. The post Lipsky cited in a New York Times article on the specter of Trump’s tariffs over G7 ministers meeting. appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 🏛️ Australia starts ‘high-risk’ life extension of its submarine fleet | Defense News
item.description: The Australian National Audit Office (ANAO) has issued a damning verdict on Department of Defence planning for the life-extension program.

item.title: 🏴 Amid FPV threat, Aussie company to offer Ukraine armored roofs for ATVs | Breaking Defense
item.description: Ballistic protection from overhead dangers come as modern militaries learn to cope with FPV killers.

item.title: 📍 Beyond satellites: Why FOG inertial navigation is the new imperative for land warfare | Breaking Defense
item.description: [Sponsored] The era of uncontested GPS dominance is over. As counterspace threats and electronic warfare redefine the battlefield, learn how Exail’s Fiber Optic Gyro (FOG) technology provides the high-end stability and autonomous “source of truth” required for land maneuver and…

item.title: 🌏 Syria joins Turkey’s Efes 2026, one of nine nations participating for first time | Breaking Defense
item.description: Bulgaria, The Netherlands, Sweden, Japan, Egypt, Poland, Vietnam, Portugal and Syria were first-time participants.

item.title: 📍 Delegation of Tariff Authority by Other Means | Just Security
item.description: After the Supreme Court limited IEEPA tariff authority, the Trump administration turned to Section 301, raising new questions about executive power, trade law, and delegation. The post Delegation of Tariff Authority by Other Means appeared first on Just Security.

item.title: ⚛️ India boosts drone warfare capability with compact missile | Defense News
item.description: Indian Defense Minister Rajnath Singh called the successful development trials a “strategic milestone” toward self-reliance in defense.

item.title: 🏴 Planning for America’s Democratic Renewal Must Start Now: Lessons from Poland | Just Security
item.description: Poland’s recovery from democratic backsliding shows how hard the process can be -- and why U.S. reformers should start planning now for lawful, durable renewal. The post Planning for America’s Democratic Renewal Must Start Now: Lessons from Poland appeared first on Just…

item.title: 📍 Despite ‘peak hype,’ orbital data centers for AI not yet ready for NatSec prime time | Breaking Defense
item.description: One expert summed up the defense market viewpoint of AI data centers in space as: “If they build it, we might come.”

item.title: 🌍 Early Edition: May 26, 2022 | Just Security
item.description: Signup to receive the Early Edition in your inbox here. A curated guide to major news and developments over the weekend. Here’s today’s news: IRAN WAR – CEASEFIRE President Trump signaled on Saturday that a deal with Iran was imminent, writing in a post on social media that…

item.title: 🏛️ An Inconvenient Reality: Climate-Preparedness Cuts Are Lethality Cuts | War on the Rocks
item.description: In 2019, the Missouri River flooded at historically high levels and damaged 137 facilities, destroyed 1.2 million square feet of workspace, and flooded 3,000 feet of runway at Offutt Air Force Base. Repairing the installation cost $1.2 billion. The Trump administration and…

item.title: 🌍 The U.S. Military Lacks an Ethics Doctrine. Combat Effectiveness Suffers | War on the Rocks
item.description: In 2004, I was a boot (translation: brand new) first lieutenant in 1st Battalion, 7th Marines at a retransmission site in the middle of nowhere, al-Qa’im, Iraq. I heard a sudden explosion and small-arms fire two kilometers away. The battalion radio net burst with chatter, with…

item.title: 🕵️ Rebuilding American Manufacturing: A Keystone for Economic Statecraft | War on the Rocks
item.description: Editor’s note: This is the ninth article in an 11-part series examining how the United States should organize, lead, and integrate economic statecraft into strategy, defense practice, and the broader national security ecosystem. The special series is brought to you by the…

item.title: 🏴 The West indulges China in its backing for Russia against Ukraine | ASPI Strategist
item.description: Since not long after Russia’s full-scale invasion of Ukraine in 2022, the democratic world has largely watched passively as China has been the main enabler of Moscow’s war effort. Despite knowing about Russia’s reliance on ...

item.title: 🌏 The G-2 Reality | Foreign Affairs
item.description: America and China cannot dominate or exclude each other.

item.title: 📍 The War in Ethiopia Isn’t Over | Foreign Affairs
item.description: Peace deals are leading to more violence.

item.title: 🌍 Another Battle Damaged KC-135 Tanker Seen Passing Through RAF Mildenhall | The War Zone
item.description: The images of the shrapnel-peppered KC-135 are another example of the damage inflicted by Iran during the now-paused war. The post Another Battle Damaged KC-135 Tanker Seen Passing Through RAF Mildenhall appeared first on The War Zone.
